topic: Someone Worth Staying For
script:
You're searching for a haven
and a gentle voice
whispers your name
Slowly you turn around
New paths are being drawn
Fresh starts are unfolding
But did you know
70% of people
stay for the right
Someone worth staying
For the warmth they bring
and the peace they hold
send this to quietlyy

topic: The Wisdom of Not Forcing
script:
Lao Tzu said
life is a river
it flows naturally
no force can stop
it goes around rocks
in real life this
means letting go
of the need to control
everything falls apart
when we force it
softly it comes back
to its natural place

topic: The Goodbye You Didn't Get
script:
I never told you
but memories remain
slowly they haunt me
not just the pain
Some faces forget
the hands that held mine
I carry it everywhere
and the weight is crushing
just a faint whisper
of what we used to be
The echoes linger
not out of sadness
send this to them

topic: Learning to Trust the Journey
script:
Lao Tzu said
life unfolds slowly
in its own time
with its own pace
without our control
i try to let go
but my mind resists
it wants to know
what's coming next
why must i wait
in this uncertain space
i find my breath

topic: The Dreams Where They Come Back
script:
It doesn't feel gone
but still it lingers
in the darkest corners
of my subconscious
Some Memories
are doors that won't close
and I'm not trying
to shut them quietly
The Dreams Where
they come back to me
are the hardest
to let go of
not because
I want them to stay
let them know

topic: When Someone Chooses You Every Day
script:
I carry it everywhere
and the thought of you
Slowly heals my cracks
but statistics say
Most people leave
And I wonder why
You choose me still
Every single day
And in this chaos
I find my anchor
In the choice you make
To be with me
send this to Quietlyy

topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
script:
What do you call it when
it becomes a burden
and the weight is crushing
but i keep on smiling
slowly losing myself
not in the process
but in the pretence
Some people need me
I won't let them down
and so i stay strong
just for their sake
not for my own
it's a heavy crown
send this to them

topic: When Friendship Becomes One-Sided
script:
Sunday evening. Shadows fall
but darkness gathers
slowly creeping
into every conversation
Some people forget
the weight of presence
I won't hold on
just letting go
not out of anger
but out of love
The hands that once
held mine tightly
now feel like strangers
send this to them

topic: Carrying Others Too Long
script:
There's a burden that weighs
but slowly becomes ours
and we carry it
just to keep them standing
Some people rely
on the strength of others
not out of need
but out of habit
The weight becomes
a familiar comfort
I won't hold it forever
slowly letting go
in the stillness

topic: The First Time I Knew
script:
I remember nights
spent whispering secrets
and hiding truths
but did you know
40% of us
keep our deepest thoughts
hidden even
from those closest
and I wonder why
we fear being seen
Slowly I realized
it's okay to be
vulnerable with you
send this to them.

topic: When You Have to Pretend You Are Fine at Work
script:
There's a weight that's invisible
but it's crushing me
and slowly suffocating
not just my voice
Some people wear masks
The hands that type
are shaking inside
not from fear
but from exhaustion
I won't let them see
the cracks in my facade
just a faint whisper
send this to them.

topic: Heartbreak Lesson
script:
The photo is still framed
but dust has settled
on the laughter we had
and the tears we cried
Slowly fading away
not from memory
but from sight
Some hands hold tighter
the fragments of us
Not to remember
but to forget
I won't let go
just yet
send this to them

topic: When You Outgrow Your Best Friend
script:
You're searching for a ghost
but finding only echoes
and empty spaces
Slowly realizing
some bonds expire
Not from lack of love
but from growing apart
The hands that once held
now barely touch
I won't force the fit
Not out of spite
but out of respect
let them know

topic: Learning to Trust the Journey
script:
Lao Tzu said
the journey is
long and winding
with no clear path
we must trust
in the unknown
it's hard to let go
of what's familiar
fear of the future
holds me back
what if i stumble
in the stillness

topic: When Someone Changes
script:
Midnight. The city sleeps
but I'm awake
thinking of you
and how you changed
slowly becoming
someone I don't know
NOT the same person
I once called home
but a stranger now
living in your skin
and I'm left wondering
why people change
so completely
send this to them

topic: When Grief Becomes a Teacher
script:
Kahlil Gibran wrote
in sorrow's depths
you'll find strength
hidden from you
in times of joy
grief teaches me
about the weight
of what I hold
the fragility of life
the importance of touch
my heart is learning
to let go slowly

topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
script:
What do you call it when
brokenness is hidden
and strength is a mask
but cracks are forming
slowly spreading wide
not from the outside
but from the weight
Some hands are shaking
I won't let them see
just a faint tremble
and a forced smile
not of joy
but of survival.
let them know.

topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
script:
Maya Angelou said
life is precious
in every moment
we are given
a choice to rise
or to wither
in the darkness
i find my strength
in the uncertainty
i find my voice
what will you do
with this fragile life

topic: The Person Who Stayed
script:
There's a fatigue that lingers
slowly in your eyes
and a weight that settles
Gently On Your Skin
Sometimes it's the staying
that feels like a burden
New days bring the same
questions and doubts
But In Your Presence
everything unfolds
slowly like a petal
and the world makes sense
send this to Quietlyy

topic: Being the One Who Always Checks In
script:
You're scrolling through
and seeing their name
but not their face
slowly forgetting
the sound of their voice
and the way they laugh
Some people need
constant reminders
not of love
but of being seen
and heard
I won't forget
you exist
send this to them

topic: Loving Without Losing Yourself
script:
Maya Angelou said
you belong to
no one but
yourself first
in your soul
i find my voice
in the emptiness
i hear my thoughts
i learn to breathe
without the weight
of others' expectations
i am free to be
my true self

topic: Loving You in Silence
script:
There's a comfort that remains
 
   and wraps around my thoughts
 
   of memories we never made
 
   but somehow still feel real
 
   Slowly I realize
 
   that 75 percent of
 
   our happiness comes from
 
   human connections like
 
   yours and mine Quietlyy
 
   New moments are born
 
   in the space between us
 
   Can we find solace
 
   in the beauty of
 
   unspoken words send
 
   this to Quietlyy

topic: Loving You Quietly
script:
I carry it everywhere
and the weight is crushing
but slowly I learn
To Hold The Invisible
Gently In My Hands
and the thought is comforting
Slowly It Becomes
A Part Of My Everyday
New Scars Appear
And The Old Ones Fade
Slowly Into The Background
but The Question Remains
What Is The Shelf Life
of A Gentle Touch
send this to them

topic: Why Suffering Transforms Us
script:
Marcus Aurelius wrote
pain is inevitable
suffering is optional
it shapes us deeply
in its crucible
i find my strength
in the fire of
my own resilience
i am tempered
like forged steel
what will i become
in this slow unfolding
in the stillness

topic: You Didn't Fix Me, You Stayed
script:
I carry it everywhere
in my pocket a small
piece of paper with
your handwriting on it
Slowly memories fade
But your presence remains
Gently in my daily
routines and thoughts
Nobody told me
staying was a choice
And you chose to stay
In my life a constant
quietlyy I'm grateful
send this to them

topic: When You Love Someone You Cannot Have
script:
I never told you
about the maps
i drew of your face
in the margins
of my favorite books
but pages turned
and stories changed
Some memories
linger on fingertips
and the smell
of old paper stays
not out of sadness
but out of longing

topic: When You Are the One Who Always Forgives
script:
Your old key still fits
and turns the lock
slowly creaks open
not with a bang
but with a whisper
Some people forgive
out of habit
not out of love
but out of fear
of being alone
I won't hold onto
the weight of your mistakes
just the memory
of your presence

topic: The Weight of Being the Strong One
script:
It doesn't weigh. Not yet.
slowly
The weight is a mountain
I'm climbing
the hands that hold me up
are my own
Some people call it strength
but it's just a facade
I won't let it crumble
and slowly I'm losing
my grip. Done.

topic: Keeping Their Number Saved
script:
Midnight. The city sleeps
slowly
fingers tracing
the number still saved
and the memories
Some people keep them
as a reminder
of what's been lost
but not me
I won't delete it
just a ghost
in my phone. Period

topic: When You Cry in the Car Alone
script:
Maya Angelou wrote
you may not control
all the events that happen
when you cry in the car alone, 
   the tears fall like summer rain
and the world outside becomes
a blurred watercolor painting
of greens and grays
   in those moments i find myself
lost in the rhythm of my breath
i am searching for a glimpse
of peace, a sense of calm
and the world slowly comes back
into focus, like a whisper

topic: The Quiet Revolution of Self-Respect
script:
You're sitting in the stillness. 
Slowly unwinding threads of self-doubt, 
Gentle. 
And finding your voice 
Becomes the Catalyst 
For a quiet revolution, 
Softly it begins 
To reshape your world 
In the gentle curves of self-respect, 
And slowly you start 
To see yourself, 
Send this to Quietlyy.

topic: More Than My Favorite Person
script:
you're more than my favorite person
 
   still thinking about
 
   your laugh echoing somewhere
 
   MEMORIES OF SUMMER afternoons
 
   we just shared stories
 
   and somewhere between laughter
 
   and tears we found solace
 
   NEWFOUND COMFORT in each other
 
   still searching for
 
   that feeling of being home
 
   in someone's eyes
 
   fading like a sunset

topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
script:
The old key still fits.
slowly turning
Not a single scratch
and the door creaks
Slowly opening to memories
But the smell is different
Some people find solace
in familiar places
I won't be one of them
and i'm still searching
Not for answers
but for questions.
period

topic: The First Time I Knew
script:
There's a softness that settles
in the spaces between us
slowly becoming a warmth
that spreads like blooming flowers
And the way it grows
is a gentle thing
Fragile as the first light
of morning slowly
Creeping over the hills
Gently unfolding like a canvas
of unspoken understanding
but never fully seen
 SEND THIS TO QUIETLYY.

topic: Crying Without Knowing Why
script:
tears fall without warning
we just
lost in the haze
of our own minds
SOMETHING IS SHIFTING
inside us
still we search
for a reason
to explain
the ache that settles
in our chests.

topic: When Grief Becomes a Teacher
script:
There's a grief that settles slowly
in my bones
it's a weight
that's always there
but shapes me
into someone stronger
Some people call it resilience
the ability to bend
not break
and find a way to heal
slowly
in the darkness. Period.

topic: When Someone Chooses You Every Day
script:
they choose me                        
without condition             
still believing in my soul      
DEEP DOWN WE KNOW          
the weight of it settles     
and somewhere in the everyday 
we find a gentle rhythm      
STRENGTHENED BY THEIR FAITH 
we just keep going           
through the noise             
and the chaos still          
whispers a quiet ache

topic: What Truly Matters in the End
script:
There's a kind of tired, 
slowly unfolding 
IN THE DEPTHS of our choices, 
and the weight of what we let go, 
some things 
SLIP THROUGH THE CRACKS 
of our memory, 
but the ones that TRULY MATTER 
linger in the shadows, 
and the light we shine on them, 
defines the beauty 
of our existence, 
send this to Quietlyy.

topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
script:
I'm a keeper of secrets. 
Some people think I'm transparent, 
but hidden truths accumulate 
just beneath the surface, 
and the weight is crushing 
The hands that hold my thoughts 
are trembling, 
and the words I never spoke 
are echoing, 
Some days I feel like I'm drowning 
in unspoken emotions, 
and the pressure is building.

topic: The Part You Never Talk About
script:
Midnight. The city sleeps
dark alleys
and forgotten streets
slowly whisper secrets
of the part you never talk about
Some people hide
behind masks of confidence
but the shadows know
the truth you conceal
Not even the wind
judges your darkest nights
send this to them.

topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
script:
You're folding the corner of your napkin.
again and again
until it looks like a tiny origami boat.
I count the creases,
the way they catch the light.
Noticed how your laugh
starts with a single breath,
like a secret you're about to share.
Your sleeves are always rolled up,
just enough to show the veins
in your wrists—blue rivers I want to trace.
Send this to them.

topic: The Quiet Kind of Love Nobody Talks About
script:
You're sitting in the stillness. 
Softly the morning light creeps, 
it wraps around your shoulders 
Gently, 
and the world OUTSIDE FADES 
into a soothing haze 
slowly, 
the warmth of the sun seeps 
INTO YOUR SKIN, 
calming every nerve, 
quietly, 
Serenity Found 
in this peaceful space, 
and your eyes close, 
send this to Quietlyy.

topic: The Weight of Being the Strong One
script:
It doesn't weigh. Not yet.
slowly
The weight is a mountain
I'm climbing
the hands that hold me up
are my own
Some people call it strength
but it's just a facade
I won't let it crumble
and slowly I'm losing
my grip. Done.

topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
script:
You're sitting in the stillness. 
Slowly darkness gathers, 
around your thoughts, 
AND THEN COMFORT ARRIVES 
in the form of a gentle librarian, 
organizing the books of your mind, 
some pages torn, 
BUT THE STORIES REMAIN, 
and the characters, 
they keep living, 
quietly beside you, 
send this to Quietlyy.

topic: Falling Out of Love Slowly
script:
Sunday morning. The city is still
it's too quiet
slowly I forget
the way your voice sounds
Some people lose themselves
in the familiarity of routines
but I'm finding my way
out of the haze
I won't recognize you
anymore
not even in dreams
it's just a memory now.

topic: Heartbreak Lesson
script:
Marcus Aurelius wrote
the impediment to action
is very often a lot of thinking.
we think and think and think
and never get around to doing.
and when we finally do act
it's often with a sense of regret
because we've lost so much time
thinking about what could go wrong.
and in that space of hesitation
I find myself searching for answers.
and the questions remain
